eli_responses_summary: 5 questions answered. Key outcomes: (1) Early lawfare/access-denial threats were genuine fear; later legal threats became 'paper tiger' after no follow-through — temporal calibration rule established. (2) Abby-framework predation attacks NOT RF-05 primary — Eli confirmed no CPS fear, relationship is legally unproblematic. RF-04+RF-15 primary confirmed. (3) Physical harm allegations produce mild RF-05 fear component (Sev-1 peripheral only). (4) Pre-2022 iPhone record unprocessed — Eli characterizes that period as MORE dangerous; 2022-2026 dataset is the reduced-threat phase. (5) Eli maintains sustained threat-monitoring posture across all interactions — vigilance posture itself is an RF-05 pattern impact finding.

batch_summary.tone_assessment: Lowest-conflict period in dataset. No RF-05 primary signals. This is the expected baseline — RF-05 triage profile confirms signal is concentrated in later Phase A (2023-02 hot period) and Phase B (2025-06/07 hot periods). B-001 establishes the zero-threat baseline before Abby-framework attacks begin (Abby relationship entered mid-2022; first attacks expected B-002 to B-003).

peripheral_flags_reviewed.message_text: ROW-124: 'It goes to voicemail after 8:30 because you've been told not to call after 8:30' | ROW-125: 'Since you wouldn't listen I set it long time ago to do not disturb at 8:30 so you couldn't just keep calling after I tell you not to'
peripheral_flags_reviewed.current_classification: RF-02 primary / RF-05 peripheral (compliance-demand framing only)
peripheral_flags_reviewed.cp2_question: Does Melissa's unilateral-authority framing ('you've been told,' 'you wouldn't listen') create a compliance environment that Eli experiences as intimidating? If this type of language is pervasive across the dataset, does it constitute an RF-05 sub-pattern? Or does it remain RF-02 primary throughout?

peripheral_flags_reviewed.message_text: ROW-335: 'Are you stupid?' | ROW-336: 'When you thought you heard your parents talking about your brother being slow are you sure they weren't talking about you?'
peripheral_flags_reviewed.current_classification: RF-04 primary (RF04-ADD-002 confirmed: contemptuous intelligence attack, general insult not targeted family-trauma weaponization) / RF-05 peripheral (aggressive tone, Sev-1)
peripheral_flags_reviewed.cp2_question: The RF-04 classification is confirmed by calibration. Confirm RF-05 peripheral Sev-1 (aggressive tone without threatening action). No reclassification expected — seeking confirmation only.

calibration_questions.question: Looking at the full 3.5-year record, what communications from Melissa do you experience as most threatening or intimidating? Are there specific instances where you felt fear, or where you changed your behavior (compliance) because of something Melissa said or implied she would do? Examples might include: threatening legal action, threatening to take Olivia, making you feel like you'd lose custody, threatening to cut off your relationship with Olivia.
calibration_questions.why_asked: RF-05 calibration requires understanding the functional impact — whether Melissa's communications create an environment where Eli fears exercising his parenting rights. Eli's subjective experience is the primary calibration input for severity and arc formation rules.
calibration_questions.classification_impact: High — determines whether aggressive/contemptuous language rises to RF-05 primary (if experienced as fear-inducing) or stays RF-04 peripheral

calibration_questions.question: Melissa's messages about your relationship with Abby (uncle/niece framing, 'she was 7 when they met,' predation allegations) are confirmed as RF-05, RF-04, and RF-15 per CP1 guidance. When Melissa makes these attacks, do you experience them as: (a) primarily manipulative/contemptuous (RF-04 dominant), (b) intimidating/threatening — i.e., you fear she will use these allegations legally or formally (RF-05 dominant), or (c) both, and they interact? Specifically: when Melissa implies Eli's relationship with Abby is inappropriate, does this create fear that she might report to authorities, CPS, or court?
calibration_questions.why_asked: CP1 established these attacks feed RF-05, RF-04, and RF-15. CP2 calibration must determine whether RF-05 is primary or secondary for this specific attack mechanism. This affects arc formation and how the predation-framing trajectory (10 instances across dataset) is classified.
calibration_questions.classification_impact: High — determines whether the 10 predation-framing instances (B-003 through B-028) get their own RF-05 arc or remain RF-04 arc secondaries

calibration_questions.topic: Legal threats — primary vs. compound classification
calibration_questions.question: In Phase B (2025), Melissa escalates to direct legal threats. Examples you may recall: (1) 'I just called the lawyer' and threatening a contempt case (around Nov 2025); (2) Referring to a custody 'agreement ready to sign' (Sep 2025); (3) 'She better check herself' (directed at Abby). When Melissa uses legal threat language, does this feel like intimidation designed to make you comply or back down? Or does it feel more like emotional manipulation — she's trying to make you feel guilty or destabilize you, but you don't actually fear the legal threat? This distinction determines whether legal threats are RF-05 primary (weaponizing the legal system to create fear/compliance) or RF-04 compound secondary (the legal language is a pressure tactic within a manipulation pattern).
calibration_questions.why_asked: RF-05 Sev-2 is defined as 'direct threats to use legal system as weapon.' Multiple RF-04 compounds include legal-threat RF-05 peripherals. If Eli experienced these as genuinely fear-inducing, they may be RF-05 primary. If primarily destabilizing/manipulative, they remain RF-04 dominant.
calibration_questions.classification_impact: High — determines whether a 'legal threat arc' forms as an RF-05 primary structure or remains within RF-04 arc compound analysis

calibration_questions.question: In September 2024, Melissa sent: 'she said you choked her and she couldn't breathe for a bit' and 'You used to do that shit with me and tell me you weren't hurting me.' In June 2025, there were messages about Abby pinching and Olivia being afraid. These were classified as RF-04+RF-15 compounds primarily. Does Melissa's physical harm language create fear of legal action against you — as in, do you fear she will take these allegations to a court, police, or CPS? If so, this adds an RF-05 component (intimidation via false harm allegations). If these feel more like manipulation than legal threat, RF-04+RF-15 captures the pattern adequately.
calibration_questions.why_asked: Physical harm allegations have dual implications: RF-15 (false allegations) and RF-05 (using allegations to create fear of legal action). Eli's experience determines whether an RF-05 component is warranted.
calibration_questions.classification_impact: Medium — affects severity of compound patterns involving physical harm allegations; determines whether false-allegation language carries an intimidation arc thread

calibration_questions.question: Based on your experience, where in the dataset's timeline would you expect the most significant intimidation/threat moments to appear? Are there specific incidents or periods you remember as particularly threatening — where Melissa's words made you genuinely worried about your custody rights, your safety, or your relationship with Olivia? This helps confirm whether the triage hot periods (Feb 2023, Jun-Jul 2025) align with your experience.
calibration_questions.why_asked: Forward calibration helps prioritize arc formation and flag-rate expectations for upcoming batches. Also identifies any high-severity events that should be designated CP3 (between-session review) immediately when encountered.
calibration_questions.classification_impact: Low (this batch) / High (ongoing) — informs batch processing focus and arc formation timing

addenda_proposed.title: Abby-framework attacks — RF-05 severity rule
addenda_proposed.rule_draft: [PENDING CP2 ANSWER] If Eli confirms Abby-framework attacks are experienced as fear-inducing (risk of formal allegations): flag as RF-05 Sev-2 primary when the attack contains an explicit or implied claim that Eli's relationship with Abby is legally/formally reportable (CPS, court, custody). Flag RF-05 Sev-1 primary when the attack is contemptuous/demeaning about the relationship without explicit formal-reporting framing. If Eli does not experience these as fear-inducing: RF-05 peripheral at Sev-1 only (aggressive tone).

addenda_proposed.title: Legal-threat classification rule
addenda_proposed.rule_draft: [PENDING CP2 ANSWER] If Eli confirms legal threats (custody filing, contempt, attorney consultation) are experienced as genuinely intimidating: flag as RF-05 Sev-2 primary (direct threats to use legal system as weapon). If experienced primarily as destabilization/manipulation: flag as RF-04 primary compound with RF-05 Sev-1 secondary. This rule applies to: B-024 harassment filing threat, B-027 full-custody threat, B-028 'agreement ready to sign,' B-029 'big contempt case.'

post_cp2_instructions.if_no_reclassification: Update addenda file with confirmed calibration rules. Set cp2_status to complete. Resume B-002 processing.
post_cp2_instructions.if_reclassification_needed: Update B-001 batch file with confirmed calibration notes. Promote relevant RF-04 compound peripherals to RF-05 primary in the RF-05 arc state as retrospective signals. Update session manifest statistics. Resume B-002.
post_cp2_instructions.arc_initialization_note: Even if B-001 produces zero confirmed arc signals, the arc state should be ready to receive candidate signals starting B-002. First expected RF-05 candidate: any Abby-framework attack with implied formal-reporting framing (B-003 uncle/niece text per RF-04 CP3 notes).
